reviews.review_text: Faithful and amazing recreation of a timeless classic. Brilliant level design, graphics have aged well and bug free (so far atleast). Just reached Xen and it's breathtaking. Can't wait for Blue Shift.

I kinda miss having games like this that don't hold your hand. Had some times where I was stuck for awhile, but it was that much more satisfying when I finally figure out the way forward. This is a very fun and rewarding game! Highly recommend.

I don't agree with the idea that this game is better than Half-Life 1 and should be played instead of it. Half-Life 1 is a considered a masterpiece for a reason and should be experienced under its own terms, especially because this game does make changes that I believe diminish the experience. Several sections of the game have been made artificially longer by, for example, making you find a valve to screw in to open a door instead of just having a button do the same thing. It's more akin to Half-Life 2's map design in a sense, but it doesn't improve the experience by being there. That being said, Black Mesa is a great game and is worth experiencing as well, but just play Half-Life 1 first. It's impossible to appreciate the changes CC made when developing the game without the proper context.

Freaking loved every single second of it I've played it over 60 hours or so but the hours won't show up cuz I mostly played it offline,had a hell of a time with my brother playing all the chapters Just wish there was more To this blessing

A love letter to perhaps the most influencial action game of all time, with every possible addition giving the universe new life. Too bad there was no amount of development time that could make the Xen levels unbearable.

Unlike the second game, the first half life doesn’t hold up great nowadays, but black mesa fixes that. This game’s gameplay, graphics, soundtrack, and story are all phenomenal and improved from the original. It’s just an even better remake of an already amazing game. Strongly recommend 9/10

Absolutely loved it. Every moment was enjoyable and I never got bored. The atmosphere, gameplay, and overall experience were amazing from start to finish. Easily one of my favorite games and definitely worth playing.
Since I don't know how to delete the many saves from Half-Life, I play Black Mesa. Great game, great stand alone game. In fact both are the best games ever. Highly recommend!
A welcome remaster of a classic. An adrenaline rush of an adventure.

A very good remake of the original HL with some caveats:
- cut content from several chapters
- long and tedious XEN part of the game:
it's beautiful, but very-very repetitive and boring gameplay. And it doesn't feel like a HL game. 
IDK why everyone hates Interloper, whilst the Gonar's Lair episode is long, boring and is in totally unnecessary length.
Also, tell me, who the F* makes a chapter about boss fight after the boss fight?
My three thoughts during the whole XEN gameplay:
- cool insight into Vort's life and travel of Humankind to the Xen
- it's very-very beautiful
- when will it finally end? I'm bored. I can't take any more of these stupid simple puzzles. There are too many of them and they're totally the same. Please, stop!

IMHO, one more "On A Rail" map would be much better than half Xen's map.

Xen feels like when in movies dad caughts his son with a cigarette and makes him smoke the whole pack.

And when I got Xen out of my system, to the other parts of the game:
- I wish the game would be a little harder
- HECU fighting is very dynamic, with inspirational combat music and lots of tactic.
- Lots of little details, BM complex feels much more alive than HL1 (it was good there too, but in BMS it's much better)

I played through this game from start to finish, and it was an amazing remake. But around halfway through the game crashes just kept happening over and over, most of the time requiring me to retry multiple times or set my graphics level to none. I also got softlocked and had some bugs in some points in Xen with a plug puzzle, forcing me to reload to previous saves a few times.

An amazing remake of the original Half-Life!  The improved graphics just add more to a game that was already incredible.  Fantastic and engaging storyline.  This is a must-play for anyone who loves FPS games.  My only wish is the developers add an option to take home a headcrab as a pet...

First experience with the Half-Life franchise and the PC side of gaming as a whole. I'm loving it cant wait to play part 2, and eventually the O.G version of HL1. A 1 of 1 experience, they don't make em like this anymore. 9.5/10
It's solid. If you want to introduce Half Life to someone who is not into older styled graphics, I genuinely view this game as a substitute. There's mod support as well. All around heavily recommend.
I really enjoyed it, even though later portions of the Xen segment are a slog - overall it feels like the game is an hour longer than it should be.

started playing in late january 2026 then almost finished it at level interloper back in late february but i couldn't find the time. that level was hard and i got help from BigMacDavies guide it's really helpful if you stuck. it was a 8/10 experience and i wanna continue to half life 2 now.
Just play the original Half Life. This port crashes more than Tiger Woods when drunk.

Exceptional. An absolute love letter to Half Life. Brings it into the modern era without sacrificing any of its charm. I still say I got rather frustrated with the level design at first, but coming back to it after beating the original Half Life: I ended up admiring the project. Truly a great experience.
good fun, good times, except for performance drops here and there, it's rock solid.
Good fan made remake, if not the BEST one out there. Just feels like it can drag on certain areas like xen, but its definitely worth it!

Good game. 7.5/10. Good remaster of a classic title. Overall the game is enjoyable but with a few caveats. Not the best balance of difficulty since the soldiers have pinpoint accuracy. The pacing is not great through the whole game, especially the xen region. The story is also barely there. The sequel is a true masterpiece since it improves everything by a magnitude.

This is genuinely the best remake for a game I've ever played. If you've played the original Half-Life, you should 100% try out this game. Also, don't play Half-Life source if you're looking for an enhanced version of Half-Life 1. Seriously, don't do it.

Overall a great expirence and a great remake of half-life, Xen being one of my favourite parts of the game. I don't know what happened in the Interloper chapter, but the Vortiguant factory is one of the worst parts of the game. Feels very game-y, looks worse than the rest and overstays it's welcome by like 2/5 of the playtime.

This was the very first game I played after I got an upgrade on my PC. It wasn't much but enough to run this game on highest and OH BOIII it delivered. Finishing the game a part of me wished to play it again in a new memory and start a fresh run with how I was blown away. One the best games I played and would recommend it especially to new gen players who want to experience Half-Life in modern iteration.
I would recommend this to new players not just because it's good but it also a good remake of half life one then after I would try half life.

Puedo decir con seguridad 3 cosas:
Es una obra maestra.

Es el mejor remake de la historia

Sin duda supero al Half Life 1 original

Y sin duda, uno de los mejores juegos que he jugado en mi vida.

I only got 4 hours on it so far but it's a very good remake of the original half life
if you like half life this is a good game to play too a lot of lame asses like to argue about if this or half life 1 is better and like man just play both of them man. both of them are good. end of man end of

I was a little sceptical at first, being an old school Half-Life-enjoyer, Not that I ever wanted to write anything negative, it is a fan game after all, it isn't Valve re-making Half-Life. I feel I've been entirely unfair, and I hadn't played since they released Xen. Holy moly. Xen.

I'm just going to keep this short and say this: Xen makes this entire game worth your time. Trust me. If you haven't played it yet, you owe it to yourself. I'm not sure what happened between the team starting the game and then them working on Xen, but they decided to reverse what Valve did and actually made it the best damn part of the game. And not just by a small margin. The enemies... the puzzles... the art and atmosphere.. the MUSIC, the soundtrack is so damn good.

Suddenly all the little criticisms I had about earlier parts of the game (making sections that were pretty good puzzles in the original game and basically breaking them by, for example, adding a pipe you can jump on which skips the whole puzzle) were completely null and void.

For a standalone game ranked against other similar games, this is easily an 8/10. But as far as Half-Life fan games/mods (I've played MANY) this is easily a 15/10. Fantastic.

If you were on the fence about whether to give it a go, do yourself a favour.
Pretty damn good, the changes made from the original are tasteful and the Xen levels were actually fun to play. 9/10

From, the short time that i have played, I really think Black mesa is, wonderful. It really is the competitor to the original half-life. I've personally enjoyed, how, dedicated the team is on black mesa and It's a fun game to play!

if you are reading, this review and haven't played Black mesa, You should. It is in my opinion, one of the best Remakes of half-life to date!

Good game that is unfortunately held down (and up) by its own creative liberties from the original. I have nothing but respect for the devs and their dedication for such a project, but for every one or two new things I really liked about this remake, there was another thing that was more irritating than welcoming. The environments of both the old and new are stunning and meticulously detailed, but the level design can sometimes feel super scatterbrained in its execution, leading to getting lost for twenty minutes a bit more often. The gunplay feels good in the hands, but, in my opinion, the enemy AI feels like a straight downgrade; more prone to mindless, jittery rushing than HL1 (most notably the Houndeyes), yet also less organized in attacking and defending than HL2. The new take on Xen is fascinating in both its visuals and firefights, but its emphasis on using its extended runtime for mostly puzzles made it WAY more grating than it ever needed to be. Still better than the original Xen, but damn, at least the original's was fairly short.

My biggest problem with Black Mesa is that it treats itself as if it's a full-blown Valve-esque singleplayer campaign, but it lacks that notable Valve polish to reinforce that claim. Even though my relationship with this game is mixed, I still recommend giving it a go if you love Half-Life. But I'll always view this game as a supplement and never a replacement.

As much as I wanted to love this I cant recommend it, based mostly on my experience on steam deck. I had near constant crashes even after switching to proton experimental, including one hard crash that kept happening in one of the last levels of the game, that stopped my progression and forced me to give up. I may go back one day and play on PC, but I am surprised this was so poorly optimised given it is a remake of the game that put Valve on the map.

On top of that, I found the gunplay weak, I mostly just "pray and spray" at a lot of the soldiers who seemed to unload entire clips at a time, making them very frustrating to fight. A lot of the levels, particularly the expanded Xen levels are also overly long and become quite tedious.

Unfortunately I would not reccommend this game to anyone new to Half Life. It should not be considered the definitive expecience. 

However when you take into consideration that this is a fan made project it becomes much more enjoyable. but most people buying may not be aware of this. Also SteamDeck performance is very uneven.

The game is awesome. I love Half LIfe series. This review isn't about the game itself, but how it performs in Linux in general. The game isn't supported on Linux natively. That's why I use the steam compatibility to run the game. Before the game starts, there is a shader pre-compilation and this takes a looong time. Luckily, this happens only when you run the game for the first time, or after an update to the game. There are updates to the game every day. Every day, the game has to do a shader compilation. Every time I play this game this happens and this becomes very annoying at some point. If this wasn't Half LIfe, I would have already requested my money back. DEVS! LINUX IS REAL NOW! PLEASE!

This is a very VERY good game and i recommend playing it... uuunnlessss you have a pc like mine and when you load the game it either crashes or you can play it for an hour then crashes your whole pc...
This game is basically a remaster/reimagining of this neat obscure game called Half-Life. It adds and improves so much, they might as well call this Half-Life 2.

This is a good remake when it works. Unfortunately, it doesn't work. Not out of the box, that is, in a lot of cases. First there's the railway crash problem (I use linux), and also a bunch of other little things. Halfway through my playthrough, the updated GUI lost its will to live and only forcing the old menu in the launch options could get the game back up again.

This is exactly what a reimagining should do.
10/10 Crowbar Collective absolutely cooked with this one.

- Me, bored, checking list of games I bought and never played years ago
- See [b] Black Mesa [/b] in the hidden depths of my library
- "Meh, could be a good time killer, for an hour or two"
- [i] 20 hours later [/i] *surprised pikachu face*

[b] WHY IS THIS GAME SO GOOD? [/b]

As someone who passively played the original on PS2 (without completing it, sadly), this game is such a good remake, with faithful recontruction of the original but with upgrades! The added interactivity, new level designs, and puzzles that gets the brain juices flowing. Story so eerie and mysterious, I'm eager and ready to find out more of the lore in the sequels!

10/10, would definitely recommend to those that want to return, to finish a timeless classic but with modern feel, or replay nostalgia in HD and with new twists!
Keeps crashing for no apparent reason. Can't recommend a product that is unplayable.

This game in specific is an amazing retelling of the original Half Life game, with some level design changes which better fits the flow of the game. lots of secrets were added, and xen was great! The developers accomplished an amazing feat in which modernizes Half Life for modern audiences. 

Despite that, the gunplay is just awful, even worse than the original Half Life gunplay. This somehow feels less responsive and tactile than a game from 1998. On top of that, all the enemies feel like bullet sponges. combat in this game feels like a chore, which is upsetting because of the amount of encounters you have in this game. This makes the game borderline unenjoyable and a pain to complete fully.

I feel like this game could've been leagues better if it tried to recreate the original game with the assets that came with the source engine by default. It would at least feel as responsive as half life 2 if they went in that direction instead of making everything custom. The experience entirely falls apart when you have gameplay like this. Another thing that annoys me is the flashlight. Why is it like that? that was a stupid change.

Excellent Half-Life remake. I know you want old-school shooter where you use your brain to navigate the map and shoot enemies of course. Overall graphics wise the game looks close to HL2 which is actually good - you could play HL2 after that and don't see much difference.

Pros:
  - The game don't think you are an idiot.
  - Good shooting sections that interleave with calm ones. This actually helps to not get tired.
  - Nice variety of enemies taken from original Half-Life.

Cons:
  - Nihilanth fight felt too long and its last phase may be randomly difficult due damage from flying rocks.

[h2]This is a movie if it was a video game, not a modernized 1990s video game[/h2]

And certainly not the Half Life we know and love.
What I mean by movie-as-a-video-game is exactly how you can envision producing a video game but having the brain of someone, that produces movies: Movie humor and scripts, characters failing or dying when they shouldn't, unneeded super suspensful music and things are either not adding up naturally or there are so many elements, that deviate away from Half Life (story wise, lore wise, what happened canonically and even whole maps and "puzzle elements" got dumbed down, shortened, changed or omitted altogether). You as the player can feel your intelligence being insulted, especially when you want to backtrack and recharge your HP, Armor and Ammunition, only to find, that the way back has been blocked off in many instances (this almost never happens in Half Life).

For example [spoiler]at the beginning of the Power Up chapter, where the player first meets a Gargantua, the usual doorway they go through is locked and you have to find a different way in, which is through the tunnel to the right (in the original you go there at the end of the chapter, not at the beginning!), climb up the power station and then climb up to the ventilation shaft to enter. And if you want to go back there AFTER dealing with Gargantua in order to recharge your Armor or whatever, you can't. The way's been blocked off.[/spoiler]

As if that wasn't enough, you as the player are severely put into a firepower and ammunition handicap, not that you can't win but it restricts you severely. All your ammunition pools (with the exception of the explosives in Slot 5, the RPG, the Tau & Gluon Cannons and the Mosquito Launcher) have been halved AT LEAST! The Crossbow got the worst treatment of all: Instead of 5 bolts in the weapon + 50 bolts in the reserve for a total of 55 shots like it is in the original, you get only 5 bolts in the weapon + 10 reserve for a total of 15 shots! And the underbarrel grenade launcher of the SMG got reduced from 10 to 3 grenades you can carry!

Firepower got weakened too. In the original Half Life you were able to easily 1 shot the Alien Zappers (Alien Slave, Vortigaunt) with a 357 Magnum or the SPAS Shotgun with a single barrel shot to the body. Here they survive that. The Alien Sonic Boomers (Houndeyes) do not flinch and get their attacks interrupted upon being hit and have a shorter fuse time, so you end up getting Sonic Boomed anyway.

Stealth gameplay does not exist here. You can't perform silent kills with the crossbow anymore and neither can you sneak up to hostile soldiers and launch a precooked surprise grenade strike.

I can say something negative about the long anticipated borderworld Xen chapter too [spoiler] and that is it's almost nothing like Xen. The levels are completely different, the perception is not an alien space world made of asteroids and "islands" but a coddled up version thereof as if it's a planet with a whole biosphere and flying rocks and things. And yet again, it became another ground for action movie entertainment elements rather than a video game you play and figure.[/spoiler]

I cannot consider this to be a modernized canon version of Half Life. A literal imitation thereof it is, like if your friend asks you if he can copy your homework and you respond "Alright but make it look like you didn't copy it off me" and he does so but then gets graded the way I grade Black Mesa (this videogame), the wannabe Half-Life imitation.

Therefore I recommend quite heavily against this video game. By skipping this, you won't really miss much unless you're a graphic wh*re or are a movie freak.
Amazing. Xen section even more amazing. Clearly a labor of love and worth your time. Purple Hat achievement, possibly not worth your time.
absoltely amazing. actually brought tears to my eyes from the sheer amount of love poured into this. valve should hire all of you
encountered a glitch where it went almost pitch black fixed and had a blast so yeah i love it and kinda hate it for the darkness glitch
Fun game trying to reimagine half life. Xen is way too long though.
I was thinking of playing it but it always crashes on the first chapter, but that’s ok, maybe one day I’ll play it without crashing

[h1]A bit of love and hate[/h1]

It's a passionate remake and I liked the original game but will never finish this one, it's just not giving me any joy
Game stutters the exact same no matter what hardware you have this game runs like ass.
i lost my back after navigating for like roughly 10 hours and xen is too confusing long

Thumbs up, but kinda barely.
Do not install on SD card, install on SSD, or the loading screens are kinda brutal. At least on SteamDeck.
Great story. 
Great visuals. 
Locked 60FPS while docked, probably higher in handheld mode. 
Engaging characters and environments, when it works. But…
It’s crashed my SteamDeck at least 5-6 times. I had to use proton experimental to even get it to run past the opening tram ride, after 2 crashes when tram ride ended.
After that it ran great an hour or more then would crash, usually at a loading screen.
Crashes seem to be longer and longer in between tho…..
Controls are kinda hit or miss, ladders drive me nuts trying to get on and off.
Weapons feel useless besides crowbar.
There’s some charm to the throwback jank feel. But I just got to where you gotta jump across the elevator shaft to reach ladder and it’s so finicky i died like 10 times and rage quit.
Also the music is not mixed well. Very loud and kinda too intense. I turned music down to 50% and thats good.
I loved portal 1&2 so much I wanted to play their other games but playing the OG Half-life feels too dated so I chose this. Im kinda determined to play through it and I think saving often and turning settings down a tiny bit will be doable. But be prepared to do a little homework with this game
Worth the 3$ on sale, obviously.

Literally the only game of the countless i have on steam that are workshop supported, where im expected to go out of my way to do all the busy work of installing myself by digging through my files. this is 2026 not 2006, i dont care what any chuds wanna say about my technical literacy when what i stated above makes this issue unique to this particular game. Was really looking foward to playing the fanmod remakes of blue shift and opposing force but its not worth the unessescary headache. ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ get your fingers out of your asses and learn how to figure out something literally every other steam workshop supported game does by default, i dont care what sob story you wanna give about humble begginnings.
Bricked my steam deck and had to get it repaired. Thanks, Crowbar interactive. I reeealy appreciate the two sleepless nights weeping about my BRAND NEW STEAM DECK.
Not great. Hasn't dated well. Play's janky. But not goldsource janky like you remember. Graphics are overated.
better than original and would suggest to people if you only care about good graphics for some reason

I absolutely do NOT recommend this game.  I cannot understand why there are so many positive reviews about this remake when there are so many problems with it.

Let's start with the performance.  It's GARBAGE.  Even on a machine well above the specs for this game, you will get constant frame drops.

Next let's go to the enemies.  They are awful.  Once triggered, they have a constant awareness of your location even when they shouldn't be able to see you.  Some of them will even be able to hit you around corners before you have a line of sight on them.  It's sloppy game design and totally unreasonable.

Xen was completely redesigned for this remake.  It's totally different.  From a visual standpoint, it is amazing.  From a gameplay perspective, it's definitely inferior to the original.  It's quantity over quality, at least in terms of the gameplay.  Thiis is not a unique criticism by any means.  The Xen portion of this game has been widely criticized.

They really missed the mark on this.  It is a disservice to the original Half-Life 1.  Don't waste your money on this thing.  Don't fall for the hype.  They spent between 1 and 2 decades working on this, and in my opinion it was a complete waste of time.  Skip it.

An old, buggy game with outdated game design and ancient-looking textures. The story is barely there, the locations are annoying, and everything feels slow, dragged out, and unnecessarily tedious.

I am not saying the developer did a bad job. It is obvious this was made by a fan, for fans, and I respect that. But for someone who has never played any of these games before, I absolutely cannot recommend it — and here is why.

You can remake the textures in Unreal Engine 5 if you want, but the core design will still be bad. When you spend five minutes searching a location for a rusty pipe made of two polygons that blends into the background just so you can move forward, that is bad design.

When the game makes you run around looking for green buttons, but half of them cannot be pressed because they are just textures, while the actual button you need looks almost the same but is hidden somewhere else — that is bad design too.

The game explains nothing, gives you no clear direction, and constantly feels like it is wasting your time. Some games from 1998 should not be remade. Let them stay dead.

Do not waste your time on this. If you are interested in the world or the story, just watch it on YouTube.

I don't recommend it. It's too difficult and frustrating to play.
I keep getting lost and don't know where to go.
Why were '90s games designed like this?
Worst ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game I have ever played. A ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ disasterous piece of abysmal ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ of a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game.
Gordon you LOUSY mother ♥♥♥♥♥♥ get your DORK ass down to the test chamber or else I will shove the sample up your ASS....AND STOP ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ WITH THE MICROWAVE

I really love the care and attention that BM has gotten in its art direction and rework, but the combat is just infuriating.  Enemies have no stagger, HECU marines lock on to you the INSTANT you are within line of sight, the shear number of unfair ambushes that the devs placed within each and every level, the innaccuracy of your own weaponry, it all makes combat feel like an absolute slog.  Certain encounters feel designed to punish the player, and if you don't know what's coming you WILL take a huge chunk off of your life only because the AI has pinpoint reaction times and accuracy.  

Marines and aliens bumrushing you even when on the brink of death breaks the satisfying combat loop of the first game.  When save-scumming becomes the best tactic for players, your combat system has failed.

I would love to recommend it, but the devs are treating this game like Unreal Tournament and not Half-Life
very unstable as of right now. crashes for every step you take

It's so good I cried. 😭
Please give us Blue Shift and Opposing Force. 🫶

A fraudulent recreation with bad stuff sprinkled in.

If you choose to play this instead of the original, shame on you.
Brings back the old fun of the original half life.

Playing this game made me sit there and think a single thought, 'WHY TF DIDNT I PLAY THIS UNTIL NOW!!'

Solid 9/10
Amazing game. Still, in 2026 gameplay is very nice

The sound design is a massive downgrade and so is the lighting - everything is too dark and/or lit in a very boring, forgettable way. Combined with claustrophobic and cluttered level geometry, and you end up with the locales that leave no lasting impression (again, another big misstep, compared to both HL1 and HL2 that you can practically run in your imagination they're so vivid)
The scientists sound like redditors, the vorts sound like drunk bums, the houndeyes look like they're made of plastic, the gibbed chunks look like the limbs have been hacked off the torso with a saw - I feel like Gordon Ramsay going "no no NO it's all WRONG!", flipping layer after layer of what was supposed to be an improved experience of a game, originally made by people that spearheaded coherent design in everything from level to gameplay as their center-core philosophy.

It's hard to find literally anything this remake does better than the original.
Half life is one the best games ever made. Its timeless. The remake is one of the best remakes ever made
It's Good And Fun! was kinda Skeptical to buy this at first but the game play and new stuff was great!

Normally i don't post a review for a game until i have fully completed everything it has to offer. However, this game was incredible and i just don't think the rating does it justice. Most of the negative reviews are for constant crashes or running poorly. I never had an issue and my pc is like 5 years old and wasn't even amazing 5 years ago.

Really good really solid. Xen is the highlight but two thirds too long overall, borderline wank material vs homage to a great game.

[spoiler] I would have kept Xen as it is but cut out most of that factory/freezer level on Black Mesa - you know it if you've played it. And probably nerf the factory bit at Xen [/spoiler]

Overall, 10/10 for anyone who never got a chance to play the orginal half life
prob the best remake ive ever played, it was a upgrade to a game that i already likes its genuinely one of the best games ive ever played 10/10

Beautful design, Amazingly smooth gameplay, Great music, A complete Xen Upgrade you could hardly think it's that xen from the original half life. I may be 17 in a few more hours, haven't played half life, but this sure as hell a 10/10 remaster to me

(also go to google and search up xen from half life and black mesa to see how both look if you haven't known)
1 of the best remakes of my child hood game i have ever played if u loved half life 1 and ur a fan or just want to play a game with: good story, visuals and mechanics buy it and play it its worth it

I never leave reviews and I cannot give this enough praise. This is hands down the best remake of any game I have ever played and possibly the best remake in my liftime. 

Thank you Valve and Developers who built this remake.

the entire game is exceptional and the rework of Zen... brillant. By far the best remake of my lifetime.

pretty good game

game runs nice on my computer at the highest settings in 1080p

i get around 80-140fps which is playable in my terms, sometimes it dips down to 30 when a lot of stuff is happening, but that happens briefly.
Kinda slow at the start but keep going it gets better. I like how this game doesnt hand hold you, theres lots of environmental clues on what to do next

Finally after a lot of years I played Black Mesa, I haven't played the original HL but this re-make(?) is awesome, it was entertaining until the very end, and iirc this was the first time that I played a game without the need for HUD or maps, which is an achievement on its own of how good it is.

So yea, please play this all time classic, it's worth of your time.

Great reimagining of a great game. Lots of spots to get lost and waste a bunch of time but you can tell the devs put their heart into the second half of the game starting with the xen section. You just can't get around the mid level design of a game built around this time period and they did a decent job at routing this better. Fun gameplay, loved the ending and especially gonarch's lair was super fun.

This is truly a good reimagining of Half-life, I dont understand why so many people think this game is garbage, even though its really a good game, and Ive never recommended a game more than this. There's a reason VALVe put this on the Steam storefront instead being a mod...
Very good remaster but some times crashes when there's too much props/entites.

Id absolutely recommend this game, worth the price, better than you could ever expect and every last detail is perfectly done, you may be stuck here and there but a little bit of exploration finds you a way, there may be some small insignificant visual bugs but they don't matter. (if you dont like the game then you just suck at half life)
10/10
mayne if i just start doin shi mayne dont even hit me up 3 days in jail for zape dont even id you not even a zorpologist dont even
If the game is too dark, try adjusting the brightness slider the other way. who on earth designed it like that

Very good remake. Not only zen has been completely redone, but a lot of the game's original sections too. Elevates the story elements in doing that, gun combat is better and all of the visuals are stunning.
9/10

Half-Life faithfully recreated in Source (or XenEngine I should say). Can it get any better? YES! Xen is a whole other game by itself. You will recognize important milestones from the original, but (thankfully) it is mostly very different and I would say a vast improvement. Xen is absolutely stunning and the added gameplay is brilliant.
ive loved every part of the game so far would recommend trophies are pretty ez too

Black Mesa: Definitive Edition is the ultimate “I can fix this” simulator for people who absolutely cannot fix this.  
It’s faithful to Half‑Life, but bold enough to improve it.
It’s cinematic, chaotic, atmospheric, and occasionally ridiculous — in the best way possible.
If Half‑Life is a classic novel, Black Mesa is the deluxe illustrated edition with better lighting, more explosions, and a crowbar that hits like a truck.

That's a great remake but you should definitely play og Half-Life before. I don't think Black Mesa managed to catch the ambiance which is so unique in the original game. Remade Xen is great but it can be boring when you replay it. The soundtrack is absolutely gorgeous. Also, Hidden Hat and Pizza achievements are so tedious and boring...

I've played the games over and over again and never get bored, i did the achievements separately just so i can experience the game and story in repeat. I spent more or less three days on this game, and i don't regret a second of  it, the gameplay, the graphics (especially xen), and the soundtracks, god, they make you feel unstoppable (at least until a marine guns you down to death). Overall, i agree with some people that you should play the OG half life before going for its remake

If you're looking for a more modern interpretation of Half-Life, this game is the closest thing you'll get to that. 

Things I liked:
-The Gameplay 
-NPC interactions 
-The graphics

The combat in this game is really fun to play around with as you have multiple different weapons to experiment with. However, my dumbass kept accidently pulling out the rocket launcher in tight spaces, leading to my death.
The Puzzles are sometimes a little confusing at times, but those issues were few and far between. Overall, they were very fun to figure out.
The friendly NPCs feel much more alive compared to the original Half-life since as it was made in a cave with a box of scraps. Also it helps that the source engine is much more powerful compared to uhh forgot the name of the half-life engine.
The Graphics are good, what else do I have to say?

Issues:
-Interactivity at some parts
-Overheating of my pc (Skill issue on my end)

The thing preventing me to love Black Mesa like I do with Half-Life 2 or the original Half-life is the lack of interactivity in some parts. For example, you can't break specific props such as military radios which makes the world seem a little less alive. Also, some props sometimes look breakable, so I get my trusty crowbar to break it. Yet, when I smack it, it seems to be made of an indestructible material.
My PC sucks so ofc it overheated.

After consistently going back to OG Half-Life/BS/OF at least once every couple of years since I was a kid, I finally decided to check out BM. I played through "We've Got Hostiles" the other night in my first sitting with it. I was dumfounded at the level of detail the team managed to cram into every nook and cranny and at the expansions they did to all the areas.

So last night a friend sent me the link to Sven Co-op, and we downloaded it to mess around with co-oping the story....and I was left utterly devastated. I can now no longer enjoy the original game after seeing the completed vision of Black Mesa. It feels so dated and barren in a way that I've never felt before when doing my regular playthroughs. I fear I'll never be able to again dedicate a full day to reliving the nostalgia of my childhood.

Thanks and f you, guys, for making such an amazing product.

Also, it runs great. I have yet to find a single bug aside from the classic auto-firing if you had the trigger held down during a zone transition from HL2. Highly recommend it unless you want to preserve your image of the OG game. And for those who HAVEN'T played the OG game yet, you would be doing yourself a MASSIVE disservice by playing this first. The OG is a touchstone experience for anyone who loves an FPS that isn't afraid to ♥♥♥♥ on you every now and then.

I have always had a preference for Half-Life 1 over 2 in terms of story and scope. Half-Life 1 is very straight-forward, an easy-to-follow story with fun and fast gameplay, but lingers for too long in some areas, and it makes revisiting the original a chore sometimes.

Black Mesa cleans up that original experience and refines it into a proper, modern game, following the incredible blueprint laid out by Valve, and fixing what didn't work before.

Let's not kid outselves, we all know Half-Life 1 was a series of ideas being thrown at the wall, and not all of them stuck. HL1 sits in the same ballpark as Halo: Combat Evolved, a very formative experience for many people and the industry, but some parts of it aged like milk. Black Mesa tweaks those parts, gives them the care they needed, and runs with its own ideas to create a familiar, yet completely fresh experience.

Black Mesa does something that was lost in the original, and has since been seemingly collectively forgotten about. Black Mesa emphasizes the horror of Half-Life 1. When you divorce yourself from the cartoonish designs and over-the-top voice acting present in the original and really put yourself into the HEV suit of Gordon Freeman, you begin to realize how actually scary the resonance cascade really was. The darker environments of the earlier sections, somber soundtrack, and even slightly slower gameplay really highlight the terror of the beginning of the game, only becoming faster and more action-oriented when the military becomes a threat, and increasing the tempo until the very end.

Xen is an amazing overhaul as well. Xen in the original felt like a rushed area of the game with not that much thought put into it, just to wrap things up. Black Mesa enhances a part of the game that was extremely lacking and very uninteresting. It very much does feel like they only started working on Xen when the rest of the game was finished so they could give it the proper amount of attention it deserves. What went from what was essentially a few basic pieces of geometry disguised as platforming puzzles, a very bland interior, a bullet sponge boss fight, and a final boss that was quickly cobbled together really now feels like its own game, adding modern elements and setpieces to really make it stand out, giving the whole thing a very unique visual identity, even clashing with the rest of the game in a way that makes sense (because it is an alien planet, after all).

Overall, I would still recommend a playthrough of Half-Life 1, but Black Mesa is just as worth the time.

Absolutely amazing game. I can feel all the love and hardwork but in by the devs and voice actors through every chapter. I espesially love the blue shift remake as Barney is my favorite character, it was amazing getting to see the unique and re-imagined storyline for blue shift and I wait with anticipation for the last few chapters, I know the wait will be worth it!

a great remake with amazing graphics, fun gameplay and a workshop. well worth the price. the only issue i have with it is that it has some performance issues and glitches at times, but other than that it's a great mod and i would highly reccomend it, though for anyone who has not played the original half life, i reccomend you play through that before downloading BM.
